questions.question: When you go to a gathering with lots of new people, you:

optionA.text: Actively introduce yourself to many people first

optionB.text: Tend to stay quiet next to someone you know

questions.question: How do you recharge your energy?

optionA.text: Hanging out and chatting with friends

optionB.text: Spending time alone

questions.question: When plans come up suddenly on the weekend, you:

optionA.text: Great! Exciting! Let's go somewhere!

optionB.text: Hmm... I was planning to rest at home...

questions.question: When having a conversation, you:

optionA.text: Organize your thoughts as you talk

optionB.text: Organize your thoughts before speaking

questions.question: Your circle of friends:

optionA.text: You know many people and have a wide network

optionB.text: You prefer a small number of deep relationships

questions.question: In a team project, you:

optionA.text: Like gathering together to discuss and brainstorm ideas

optionB.text: Prefer each person taking a part and working individually

questions.question: When you get a phone call, you:

optionA.text: Answer right away and enjoy the conversation

optionB.text: Would rather reply by text or message if possible

questions.question: When studying at a café, your preferred environment is:

optionA.text: A lively spot with a moderate amount of people and noise

optionB.text: A quiet, secluded corner seat

questions.question: When you have a problem, you:

optionA.text: Talk it through with a friend to sort it out

optionB.text: Think it over carefully on your own

questions.question: In a conversation with an acquaintance you haven't seen in a while:

optionA.text: Words come naturally and you lead the mood

optionB.text: Awkward silences make you uncomfortable and you have to search for things to say

questions.question: When it comes to travel:

optionA.text: You prefer a group trip with several people

optionB.text: You want to go alone or quietly with one or two close friends

questions.question: After spending a whole day socializing with people, you:

optionA.text: Feel recharged and even more energized

optionB.text: Feel drained and need recovery time alone

questions.question: When making travel plans, you:

optionA.text: Plan the detailed itinerary, places, and times carefully

optionB.text: Decide only the general direction and wing it as you go

questions.question: Your preferred way of learning something new:

optionA.text: Practice step by step in a structured way

optionB.text: Understand the overall concept first and then apply it

questions.question: The kind of conversation topic you're more drawn to:

optionA.text: Real experiences, facts, and practical matters

optionB.text: Ideas, possibilities, and conversations about the future

questions.question: When working, you:

optionA.text: Tend to follow proven methods

optionB.text: Tend to try new approaches

questions.question: What describes you better:

optionA.text: A realistic and practical person

optionB.text: An imaginative and creative person

questions.question: When reading a manual, you:

optionA.text: Read carefully from start to finish in order

optionB.text: Skim quickly for only the parts you need

questions.question: When watching a movie, you:

optionA.text: Focus on the plot and details

optionB.text: Try to find hidden meanings or symbolism

questions.question: When a problem comes up, you:

optionA.text: Look for solutions based on past experience

optionB.text: Seek creative solutions from a fresh perspective

questions.question: When writing a report or essay, you:

optionA.text: Emphasize concrete figures and factual evidence

optionB.text: Present the big picture and vision first

questions.question: When cooking, you:

optionA.text: Follow the recipe precisely

optionB.text: Add ingredients by feel and make it your own way

questions.question: When thinking about the future, you:

optionA.text: Focus on realistic plans and achievable goals

optionB.text: Enjoy imagining diverse possibilities and dreams

questions.question: When choosing a gift, you:

optionA.text: Choose something practical that can be used right away

optionB.text: Choose something meaningful with a special story behind it

questions.question: When a friend shares a worry, you:

optionA.text: Analyze objectively and suggest solutions

optionB.text: Empathize with their feelings and comfort them

questions.question: The basis for making important decisions:

optionA.text: Logical analysis and rational judgment

optionB.text: Considering relationships, emotions, and values

questions.question: When giving feedback, you:

optionA.text: Tell it like it is, focused on the facts

optionB.text: Say it gently, mindful of the other person's feelings

questions.question: In a conflict situation, you:

optionA.text: Resolve it by determining who is right and wrong

optionB.text: Look after each other's feelings first

questions.question: The compliment that feels better to receive:

optionA.text: You're really good at your work — you're so capable

optionB.text: You're such a warm and wonderful person

questions.question: When the other person's opinion doesn't hold up logically in a debate:

optionA.text: Counter immediately and point out the logical flaw

optionB.text: Bring it up indirectly, considering the atmosphere

questions.question: When someone on the team makes a mistake:

optionA.text: Analyze the cause and focus on preventing recurrence

optionB.text: Comfort them first and then work on solving it together

questions.question: After watching a movie:

optionA.text: Evaluate the plot structure and logical completeness

optionB.text: Talk about the touching scenes and the emotions you felt

questions.question: When a friend is struggling after a breakup:

optionA.text: Give practical advice and talk about plans going forward

optionB.text: Cry with them and share in their feelings

questions.question: What matters more — fairness or consideration:

optionA.text: Fairness — rules and principles come first

optionB.text: Consideration — the situation and the person come first

questions.question: To you, a good leader is someone who:

optionA.text: Makes rational and fair judgments

optionB.text: Understands members' emotions well and motivates them

questions.question: When you regret a choice, the thought that crosses your mind:

optionA.text: I should have analyzed it more carefully

optionB.text: I should have followed my heart more

questions.question: Your attitude toward being on time:

optionA.text: Always keep to the time and arrive early

optionB.text: Flexible about time and occasionally late

questions.question: When you have things to do, you:

optionA.text: Make a list and check things off one by one

optionB.text: Handle things flexibly as the situation develops

questions.question: As a deadline approaches, you:

optionA.text: Tend to finish ahead of time

optionB.text: Hit a burst of concentration right before the deadline

questions.question: In everyday life, you:

optionA.text: Live in a regular, planned way

optionB.text: Live freely and spontaneously

questions.question: When making a decision, you:

optionA.text: Decide quickly and get moving

optionB.text: Keep options open and decide slowly

questions.question: When it comes to keeping your desk or room tidy:

optionA.text: It has to be organized at all times for you to feel comfortable

optionB.text: You have your own kind of organized chaos

questions.question: When you go shopping, you:

optionA.text: Decide in advance what you need and buy it efficiently

optionB.text: Browse around and spontaneously buy what catches your eye

questions.question: When something unexpected happens on a trip:

optionA.text: Feel stressed that the plan has gone off track

optionB.text: See it as a new experience and find it fun

questions.question: When starting a project, you:

optionA.text: Set the full plan first and proceed step by step

optionB.text: Start first and adjust as you go

questions.question: On a packed day, if a friend suddenly asks to meet:

optionA.text: Tell them you have prior plans and suggest another time

optionB.text: Adjust your schedule a little and meet anyway

questions.question: When it comes to long-term goals:

optionA.text: Set the goal and plan out the detailed timeline

optionB.text: Fix only the direction and move forward flexibly

questions.question: When wrapping up your day, you:

optionA.text: Organize what you need to do tomorrow before going to sleep

optionB.text: Think about tomorrow tomorrow, and go to sleep relaxed

ISTJ.title: The Logistician
ISTJ.desc: A thorough and dependable type grounded in facts. The icon of responsibility who sees every task through to the end. Prefers systematic and organized environments. Always keeps promises, values tradition and rules, and quietly fulfills their duties.
strengths: Strong sense of responsibility to follow through on commitments
weaknesses: Can be awkward at emotional expression
Can get overly fixated on rules

ISFJ.title: The Defender
ISFJ.desc: A warm and dedicated protector. Quietly fulfills their role while attentively caring for those around them. Though outwardly reserved, they have a strong inner will and deep affection for people.
strengths: Ability to identify others' needs with care
High dedication and sense of responsibility
Creates a stable and warm atmosphere
Excellent memory and powers of observation

INFJ.title: The Advocate
INFJ.desc: An idealistic yet resolute personality. Seeks to make the world a better place through deep insight, and possesses strong intuition and empathy. Though outwardly quiet, a passion for changing the world burns within — a rare type.
strengths: Pierces to the essence through deep insight and intuition
Inspires others through strong convictions and values
weaknesses: Burnout from the gap between reality and ideals

INTJ.title: The Architect
INTJ.desc: An independent and strategic thinker. Holds high standards and constantly pursues improvement — the most independent and resolute of all 16 types. Excels at understanding and designing complex systems, and cannot stand inefficiency.
strengths: Ability to form long-term strategies and vision
weaknesses: Can be insensitive to others' emotions
Can be stubborn about their own methods

ISTP.title: The Virtuoso
ISTP.desc: A curious and practical problem-solver. Understands the world by touching and analyzing things directly. Stays calm in crisis and quickly finds efficient, logical solutions.
strengths: Outstanding ability to handle crisis situations
Excellent dexterity with machines and tools
weaknesses: Can be awkward at emotional expression

ISFP.title: The Adventurer
ISFP.desc: A flexible and charming artistic type. Lives in the moment and cherishes their own space and freedom. Though outwardly quiet and shy, they have strong values and rich sensitivity within.
strengths: Excellent aesthetic sense and artistic expression
The acceptance to take others as they are
The ability to fully enjoy the present moment
A warm heart shown through action

INFP.title: The Mediator
INFP.desc: A romantic who dreams of an ideal world. Lives an authentic life guided by deep inner values, and possesses rich imagination and empathy. Has a rich inner world and a talent for expressing emotions through writing or art.
strengths: Ability for writing and artistic expression
weaknesses: Can be frustrated by the gap between ideals and reality

INTP.title: The Logician
INTP.desc: The owner of endless intellectual curiosity. Enjoys analyzing and exploring complex theories and ideas, and is highly skilled at logical thinking. Quiet and reserved, but shows more than expert-level passion in areas of interest.
strengths: The ability to easily understand complex concepts
weaknesses: Pursuit of perfect theory can delay action

ESTP.title: The Entrepreneur
ESTP.desc: An energetic adventurer! Enjoys the present moment and solves problems with quick judgment. Has a bold personality that embraces challenges without fear, and the flexibility to adapt in any situation.
strengths: Outstanding crisis response and quick thinking
weaknesses: Prefers spontaneous action over long-term planning

ESFP.title: The Entertainer
ESFP.desc: A sociable and lively mood-maker! Brings joy to the people around them and lives life to the fullest in the moment. A born entertainer who turns any occasion into a warm and cheerful atmosphere.
strengths: Positive energy that enjoys the present moment

ENFP.title: The Campaigner
ENFP.desc: A passionate, creative free spirit! Discovers new possibilities and inspires the people around them. Connects with the world through endless curiosity and a warm heart — the icon of positivity who finds the good in everyone.
strengths: The ability to inspire and motivate people
Optimism that finds possibility in any situation
weaknesses: Changing interests frequently makes it hard to finish things
Excessive idealism can create a gap with reality

ENTP.title: The Debater
ENTP.desc: An innovator who loves challenges! Challenges conventional thinking and enjoys sharp intellectual debates. Shines in any debate with a quick mind and sense of humor, and finds possibilities in what seems impossible.
strengths: Quick thinking and sharp analytical ability
Ability to learn quickly in any field
weaknesses: Good at starting but weak at finishing
Can damage relationships by enjoying arguments too much
Tends to try to solve emotional issues with logic alone

ESTJ.title: The Executive
ESTJ.desc: A systematic and practical manager! Values rules and order, and leads organizations with a high sense of responsibility. Makes decisions quickly with strong execution, and handles things with clear standards.
weaknesses: Can impose their own methods on others

ESFJ.title: The Consul
ESFJ.desc: A warm and sociable master of harmony! Quickly identifies the needs of those around them and actively helps. Places great importance on bonds with people and works to create a harmonious and stable environment.
strengths: Mediation ability that leads harmony within organizations

ENFJ.title: The Protagonist
ENFJ.desc: A charismatic leader! Finds the greatest fulfillment in discovering people's potential and growing together. Moves people's hearts with outstanding empathy and communication, and takes action for a better world.
strengths: The ability to discover and develop others' potential

ENTJ.title: The Commander
ENTJ.desc: A visionary strategic leader! Excels at pursuing efficiency and leading teams toward goals. Overcomes any obstacle with strong will and decisiveness, and is a born commander who sees the big picture and drives organizations to success.
